sourceTitle: OpenClaw release and plugin surface update (2026-03-31)

content.conciseSummary: The digest frames OpenClaw as a fast-moving developer tool whose latest release still centers model integration, Control UI, and plugin compatibility, while the plugin SDK baseline is still changing underneath custom integrations.

keyEntities.name: OpenClaw
keyEntities.description: The product being tracked across releases, commits, and workflow impact notes.

keyConcepts.name: OpenClaw release cadence
keyConcepts.description: Frequent release checkpoints that consolidate scattered OpenClaw changes into upgrade moments.

keyConcepts.name: Plugin compatibility
keyConcepts.description: The compatibility surface for plugins, custom integrations, and MCP-style extensions.

keyConcepts.name: Control UI
keyConcepts.description: A repeatedly mentioned OpenClaw surface that appears alongside CSP and model-integration changes.

keyConcepts.name: Provider configuration
keyConcepts.description: The provider-side setup and model access path that maintainers need to keep aligned with releases.

majorClaims.text: OpenClaw is shipping rapid release checkpoints while still refining model integration, Control UI, and plugin compatibility.
majorClaims.rationale: The release note is framed as another update in an already active line of changes, with the same product surfaces still moving.

majorClaims.text: Plugin SDK baseline changes can force custom plugins and integrations to adjust compatibility layers.
majorClaims.rationale: The digest explicitly connects plugin SDK baseline changes to plugin interfaces, runtime detection order, and compatibility layers.

openQuestions: How stable are OpenClaw plugin interfaces between releases?
Which Control UI and provider-doc changes actually require operator intervention?

possibleTargetPageHints.title: OpenClaw
possibleTargetPageHints.rationale: The source repeatedly treats OpenClaw itself as the stable anchor for releases, commits, and workflow impact.

possibleTargetPageHints.title: OpenClaw release cadence
possibleTargetPageHints.rationale: The release is described as part of a broader cadence of closely spaced upgrade checkpoints.
